compositionCheck.summary: All 5 variations render on the test page with correct overall structure. 7 block instances found across Blog (x3), Team Members, Resources, Policies, and Testimonials variations. The major layout elements (header, filters, grid, cards, pagination) are present and positioned correctly. However several visual issues exist at the detailed level.

issues.figmaValue: Anton Regular, 40px (font/size/3xl), line-height 1.1
issues.implementationValue: text-header-4 which resolves to 24px at >=768px (text-2xl). Anton font, line-height 1.2
issues.difference: 16px smaller than Figma design
issues.recommendation: Change from text-header-4 to text-header-3 (32px) or ideally text-header-2 if available at 40px. The Figma design uses font/size/3xl which is 40px. The closest theme token would be text-header-3 (32px at desktop) but this is still 8px off. Consider adding a text-header-3.5 or using a custom size.
issues.verification: Team member name should be around 40px on desktop, matching the bold Anton heading in the Figma team card

issues.figmaValue: Anton Regular, 24px (font/size/xl), line-height 1.15, NOT italic in the font-style sense - Anton has its own italic variant
issues.implementationValue: text-body font-bold italic - which renders as General Sans (body font), bold weight, italic. This is completely wrong font family.
issues.difference: Wrong font family (General Sans vs Anton), wrong styling approach
issues.recommendation: The role text in Figma uses Anton Regular at 24px. It should use text-header-5 (which maps to Anton at 24px at desktop). Remove font-bold and italic classes.
issues.verification: Role text should be in Anton font at 24px, not italic General Sans

issues.element: Team member card - Image container height
issues.figmaValue: Image container: 349px height with 16px padding, image with 12px border-radius inside
issues.implementationValue: Aspect ratio of 395/317 on image, padding p-4 pb-0 on container. The image container has no fixed height, relying on aspect ratio.
issues.difference: In the Figma design, image container has a fixed 349px height. The implementation uses aspect-ratio which makes the image size dependent on card width, resulting in roughly proportional but potentially different heights.
issues.recommendation: The image aspect ratio 395/317 (~1.246) combined with the padding should produce a similar visual result. The observed visual appears close but the image in implementation appears slightly taller relative to the card. Verify the image container height matches approximately 349px at the expected card width (~427px).
issues.verification: Image container should have padding on all 4 sides (16px) with rounded-xl image inside

issues.element: Team member card - Description section padding
issues.figmaValue: Description section: padding 24px (margin/margin-2) all sides, gap 24px between elements
issues.implementationValue: p-6 pt-4 with gap-2 (8px) between elements
issues.difference: Gap between card content elements is 8px vs 24px in Figma (16px difference)
issues.recommendation: Change gap-2 to gap-6 (24px) to match the Figma spacing between department tag, name, role, and CTA elements.
issues.verification: Content area gap between elements should be 24px

issues.figmaValue: 25px border-radius on card, 12px on inner image
issues.implementationValue: rounded-2xl (16px) on card, rounded-xl (12px) on inner image
issues.difference: 9px on outer card radius
issues.recommendation: Change rounded-2xl to rounded-3xl (24px) which is closer to the 25px Figma value (1px difference, within tolerance).
issues.verification: Card corners should be approximately 24-25px radius

issues.element: Blog post card - Title heading size
issues.figmaValue: 31px font-size in the blog card (close to font/size/3xl behavior at card scale)
issues.implementationValue: text-header-4 resolves to text-2xl (24px) at >=768px
issues.difference: 7px smaller than Figma
issues.recommendation: The blog card title in Figma appears at 31px. The closest available token is text-header-3 (32px at desktop). Change to text-header-3.
issues.verification: Blog card title should be approximately 30-32px on desktop

issues.element: Blog post card - Content area gap
issues.figmaValue: 24px gap between tag, title, and date elements
issues.implementationValue: gap-2 (8px) with py-6 padding on content area
issues.difference: 16px gap difference
issues.recommendation: Increase gap from gap-2 to gap-4 or gap-6 to better match the Figma spacing between content elements.
issues.verification: Blog card content gap between tag, title, and date should be closer to 24px

issues.element: Policy card - Border and corner radius
issues.figmaValue: Border: 1px solid off-white (#fbf6e2), border-radius: 25px, padding: 24px, bottom padding extra 32px below content
issues.implementationValue: rounded-2xl (16px), border-white/20 (white at 20% opacity)
issues.difference: 9px on border-radius, border color is semi-transparent white vs solid off-white
issues.recommendation: Change rounded-2xl to rounded-3xl (24px, close to 25px). Change border-white/20 to border-off-white for the correct border color.
issues.verification: Policy cards should have 24-25px rounded corners with off-white border

issues.element: Resource card - Border and corner radius
issues.figmaValue: Border: 1px solid off-white (#fbf6e2), border-radius: 25px, padding: 24px
issues.implementationValue: rounded-2xl (16px), border-white/20
issues.difference: Same as policy card - 9px on radius, wrong border color
issues.recommendation: Change rounded-2xl to rounded-3xl. Change border-white/20 to border-off-white.
issues.verification: Resource cards should have 24-25px rounded corners with off-white border

issues.figmaValue: Anton Regular, 24px (font/size/xl), line-height 1.15
issues.implementationValue: text-header-5 which maps to Anton 24px (text-2xl at >=768px) - this is correct
issues.difference: 0px - matches
issues.recommendation: No change needed. Resource card title correctly uses text-header-5.

issues.figmaValue: First card (active/featured) shows neon green (#d4ff45) background
issues.implementationValue: group-hover:bg-accent-lighter which maps to neon-green-lighter (#eeffb5)
issues.difference: Hover uses lighter variant instead of full accent color
issues.recommendation: The Figma design shows the active/featured card with full neon green. The hover state should likely use bg-accent instead of bg-accent-lighter for a stronger visual match.
issues.verification: Resource card hover should show full accent color background

issues.element: Policy card - Active state uses accent (neon green) background
issues.figmaValue: Active/featured card has neon-green (#d4ff45) background with blue border
issues.implementationValue: group-hover:bg-accent group-hover:border-accent - uses accent color which defaults to neon-green. This appears correct.
issues.difference: 0 - correct behavior
issues.recommendation: No change needed. Policy card hover correctly uses accent color.

issues.figmaValue: Policies: 32px column gap (margin/margin-3), 32px row gap. Team Members: 16px column gap (margin/margin-1), 24px row gap. Blog: cards arranged in a masonry-like alternating layout with image top/bottom.
issues.implementationValue: gap-x-10 (40px) gap-y-12 (48px) for all variations
issues.difference: Column gap: 8-24px too wide depending on variation. Row gap: 16-24px too large.
issues.recommendation: The grid gaps need to be variation-specific. For Policies: gap-x-8 (32px) gap-y-8. For Team Members: gap-x-4 (16px) gap-y-6 (24px). For Blog: similar adjustments. Consider making gap configurable per variation or using CSS that targets data-post-type.
issues.verification: Grid gaps should be approximately 32px for policies/resources and 16px for team members

issues.figmaValue: 25px border-radius on the dark background container
issues.implementationValue: rounded-3xl (24px) via before pseudo-element
issues.difference: 1px - within tolerance
issues.recommendation: No change needed. 24px vs 25px is within the 2-4px tolerance.

issues.figmaValue: No Figma reference provided for testimonial variation
issues.implementationValue: Section renders with heading only, no cards visible. The section heading 'What People Are Saying' is displayed but no testimonial cards appear (likely no testimonial posts exist in the database).
issues.difference: N/A - no test data
issues.recommendation: No design issue. The empty state shows 'No results found' text. Consider adding test testimonial posts for complete QA.

issues.element: Team member card - Image container bottom padding
issues.figmaValue: Image container and description section are both separate elements with their own bg-blue and rounded-25px. The image container has 16px padding on all sides. There is NO gap between image container and description - they overlap/butt against each other.
issues.implementationValue: Image container has p-4 pb-0, then description has p-6 pt-4. This creates a 0px bottom padding on image and 16px top padding on description = 16px gap total between image and description content.
issues.difference: The Figma design shows image and description as seamless sections of the same blue card. The implementation appears to match this visually since both have bg-accent background.
issues.recommendation: The visual effect appears correct since both sections share the same background color, making the padding difference invisible. No change needed.

issues.figmaValue: Tag: bg rgba(255,255,255,0.8), border: blue (#47cbf2), text: charcoal, Roboto Medium 14px uppercase, padding 8px, rounded-full
issues.implementationValue: bg-charcoal/10 text-charcoal, no visible border. Text is text-body-small uppercase font-medium.
issues.difference: Background color is charcoal at 10% opacity vs white at 80% opacity. Missing border. Visually the tag background appears much darker/different from the Figma translucent white.
issues.recommendation: Change bg-charcoal/10 to bg-white/80 and add border border-accent classes to match the semi-transparent white background with accent-colored border in Figma.

change.from: bg-charcoal/10 text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full
change.to: bg-white/80 border border-accent text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full

issues.verification: Department tag should have translucent white background with accent-colored border

issues.element: Blog post card - CTA Learn More spacing
issues.figmaValue: Large gap (96px, margin/margin-7) between content area and Learn More CTA at bottom
issues.implementationValue: mt-auto pt-4 which pushes CTA to bottom with 16px top padding
issues.difference: mt-auto will push to the bottom of the flex container. The pt-4 provides 16px minimum spacing above CTA. In Figma the CTA sits at the very bottom of the card with significant space. The mt-auto approach achieves the same visual result when cards are in a grid of equal height.
issues.recommendation: The mt-auto approach is correct for flexible layouts. No change needed as it achieves the same visual effect as the fixed 96px gap in Figma.

issues.figmaValue: First card (active) has tag with: bg rgba(255,255,255,0.8), border blue (#47cbf2). Default cards have: bg neon-green-20 (#eeffb5), border neon-green-40 (#ddff6a)
issues.implementationValue: bg-accent-light text-charcoal (maps to neon-green-light #ddff6a), no visible border distinction between active and default states
issues.difference: The tag background uses accent-light instead of accent-lighter (neon-green-20). Missing border on tags.
issues.recommendation: Add border border-accent-light to policy card tags. The background color bg-accent-light maps to neon-green-light (#ddff6a) but Figma shows neon-green-20 (#eeffb5) which is accent-lighter. Consider changing to bg-accent-lighter border border-accent-light.

change.from: bg-accent-light text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full
change.to: bg-accent-lighter border border-accent-light text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full

issues.verification: Policy tags should have lighter green background with border

issues.figmaValue: Tags: bg neon-green-20 (#eeffb5), border neon-green-40 (#ddff6a)
issues.implementationValue: bg-accent-light (neon-green-light #ddff6a) without border
issues.difference: Wrong background shade and missing border
issues.recommendation: Same fix as policy cards: change to bg-accent-lighter border border-accent-light.

change.from: bg-accent-light text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full
change.to: bg-accent-lighter border border-accent-light text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full

change.from: bg-accent-light text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full
change.to: bg-accent-lighter border border-accent-light text-charcoal text-body-small uppercase font-medium tracking-wider px-2 py-1.5 rounded-full

issues.verification: Resource tags should match the lighter green (#eeffb5) background with green border

issues.figmaValue: Anton Regular, 24px (font/size/xl), line-height 1.15
issues.implementationValue: text-header-5 which resolves to Anton, 24px at desktop, line-height 1.2
issues.difference: 0.05 line-height difference (1.15 vs 1.2)
issues.recommendation: No change needed. The 0.05 line-height difference is within tolerance and produces negligible visual difference.

issues.figmaValue: Load More button shown at bottom of Blog archive
issues.implementationValue: Test page has both load-more and pagination mode instances for Blog. Section 5 (Blog) uses pagination mode (shows 'Showing X to Y of Z entries' with page navigation). Section 0 and 6 (Students in Their Own Words) use load-more mode.
issues.difference: Both modes are tested, which is appropriate for QA
issues.recommendation: No issue. Both pagination modes are correctly implemented.

positiveFindings: Overall block structure matches Figma - header area with heading + description in two columns is correct
Filter bar layout with taxonomy dropdowns, Clear All, Show/Per Page controls positioned correctly
Blog post card alternating layout (image top for odd, image bottom for even) working via CSS nth-child rule
Blog post card clip-path (notched/ticket corners) correctly implemented on images
Policy card hover state transitions (background, border, text color) are implemented
Resource card hover state transitions are implemented
Team member image zoom-on-hover effect is present (scale 1.05 transition)
All filter form elements are functional (taxonomy selects, search input, per-page selector)
Active filter pills with remove buttons are correctly implemented
Loading spinner SVG is present for async loading states
Dark theme context (text-white on dark background) correctly applied
ThemeHeading component used for consistent heading pattern across variations
Pagination and load-more modes both implemented and working
Cards are wrapped in links making entire card clickable
Appropriate aria-labels on form and section elements

responsiveObservations.375w: Cards stack to single column. Heading text scales down appropriately. Filter dropdowns expand to full width on small screens (max-sm:w-full). Overall layout is functional but some sections appear quite long vertically.
responsiveObservations.768w: Grid switches to 2-column layout (sm:grid-cols-2). Heading and description still in two-column layout via ThemeHeading columns=2. Filter bar wraps appropriately.
responsiveObservations.1024w: Grid switches to 3-column layout (md:grid-cols-3). All elements properly positioned. Layout closely matches Figma desktop design proportions.
responsiveObservations.1440w: Full desktop width. 3-column grid with proper card proportions. Matches the Figma design intent most closely at this breakpoint.

summary.verdict: FAIL - 9 major issues found, primarily around typography sizing (team member name and role use wrong font/size), card border-radius (16px vs 25px Figma), tag styling (wrong background shade, missing borders), and grid gap sizing. The overall structure and layout approach is sound, but the detailed styling needs corrections to match the Figma designs.
